content: The Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM) calculates expected return based on systematic risk. Formula: E(R) = Rf + β(Rm - Rf), where Rf is risk-free rate, β measures sensitivity to market returns, and (Rm - Rf) is market risk premium. Beta above 1 means higher volatility than market; below 1 means lower. CAPM assumes investors hold diversified portfolios, so only systematic (non-diversifiable) risk matters. Despite criticisms—empirical anomalies, unstable betas, unrealistic assumptions—CAPM remains widely used for cost of equity calculations, capital budgeting, and performance evaluation. Fama-French three-factor model extends CAPM by adding size and value factors, improving explanatory power.
